sourceBlocks.title: Set up SAML SSO with Okta

capabilityFacts.value: HeyGen exposes a prompt-to-video flow through Video Agent.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The quick start documents a one-prompt to one-video flow using the Video Agent endpoint.

capabilityFacts.value: The platform supports public avatars, photo avatars, and digital twin style custom avatars.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The avatar creation guide lists Photo Avatars, Public Avatars, and Instant Avatars or Digital Twins as supported video-creation paths.

capabilityFacts.value: HeyGen supports real-time streaming avatars for virtual assistants and simulations.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The Streaming API overview describes low-latency interactive avatars over WebRTC for assistants and training simulations.
capabilityFacts.scope: Available through API and SDK paths rather than only in the editor.

capabilityFacts.value: Translated videos can be generated into 175+ supported languages with lip sync and batch outputs.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The translation API and localization page both describe multi-language video translation, batch outputs, natural voice cloning, and lip-sync preservation.

capabilityFacts.value: Localization workflows can use brand voice, term-base style controls, and asset kits.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The localization page calls out Brand Kit and Voice, while pricing lists brand voice and brand kit on paid plans.

capabilityFacts.value: Free exports at 720p, Creator at up to 1080p, and Pro or higher at up to 4K.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The pricing table explicitly maps export resolution to plan tier.

capabilityFacts.value: Watermark removal is not included on free web or API tiers but is available on paid tiers.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The pricing page shows watermark removal as a paid-plan feature, and the API plan article states that API free content carries a watermark while Pro removes it.

capabilityFacts.value: Free plan videos are capped at 3 minutes.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The pricing comparison lists 3-minute max duration for the free tier.

capabilityFacts.value: Avatar IV generation is capped to 30 seconds on free and 3 minutes on Creator and Pro.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The pricing table breaks out Avatar IV generation minutes and per-video duration by tier.

capabilityFacts.value: Legacy Interactive Avatar is being replaced by LiveAvatar and the old path is sunsetting on March 31, 2026.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The streaming-avatar docs warn that Interactive Avatar is sunsetting and instruct developers to integrate on LiveAvatar instead.

modelSupportItems.scope: Web app and API avatar video generation
modelSupportItems.evidenceText: Avatar docs say creators can choose avatar III or avatar IV, and pricing gates Avatar IV usage by tier.
The avatar creation guide explicitly says creators can choose avatar III or avatar IV depending on needs.

modelSupportItems.scope: Prompt-native video generation in the web app
modelSupportItems.evidenceText: Pricing and quick-start materials describe Video Agent as HeyGen's prompt-to-video path and gate the latest model to paid tiers.

modelSupportItems.scope: Voice selection and auto engine choice
modelSupportItems.evidenceText: HeyGen's academy material says voices are powered by multiple voice engines and that Auto picks the best one based on language and content.
The public official pages reviewed do not expose a named third-party model catalog or provider picker comparable to model marketplaces.

uniqueValueItems.summary: HeyGen is strongest where a realistic speaker, cloned persona, or interactive presenter is the center of the workflow rather than a generic scene generator.
uniqueValueItems.evidenceText: Across pricing, avatar docs, and localization pages, HeyGen repeatedly packages generation around avatars, digital twins, translation, and live avatar experiences.

uniqueValueItems.summary: The product differentiates by turning localization into a first-class workflow with batch translation, voice preservation, lip sync, and brand controls.
uniqueValueItems.evidenceText: The localization feature page and translation API are much more detailed than generic subtitle-only support pages, indicating that translated video operations are a core motion.

differentiationItems.positioning: avatar platform with adjacent prompt-to-video and localization layers
differentiationItems.evidenceText: The official materials anchor around avatars, digital twins, live avatar, translation, and business collaboration before generic editing depth.

differentiationItems.positioning: interactive avatars, digital twins, and brand-safe multilingual video are stronger headline motions than deep timeline editing.
differentiationItems.evidenceText: The reviewed pages emphasize avatar engines, translation, LiveAvatar, and brand voice; they do not emphasize frame-level editing as the core reason to buy.

collaborationItems.value: Business tier adds workspace collaboration, video draft commenting and editing, invites, and team management.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: The business plan feature list explicitly includes workspace collaboration and draft commenting or editing.

collaborationItems.value: Business adds paid extra seats, while individual tiers are single-seat.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: The pricing page lists one seat on Free, Creator, and Pro, and paid add-on seats on Business.

collaborationItems.value: Role controls, content access controls, and granular content access controls are reserved for higher plans.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: These controls are listed in the workspace and privacy comparison rows of the pricing page.

collaborationItems.value: SAML SSO is available for business or enterprise workspace security flows.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: The pricing page includes SAML or SSO in higher tiers and the academy article provides an Okta setup path inside the HeyGen workspace settings.

developerItems.value: HeyGen separates direct API billing from web-plan billing, with API keys for direct API and OAuth for MCP.
developerItems.evidenceText: The quick start distinguishes Direct API and MCP, including different auth and billing sources.

developerItems.value: Developers can programmatically translate into one or more target languages and poll translation status.
developerItems.evidenceText: The translation API docs show batch translation using output_languages and a follow-up status check flow.

developerItems.value: A dedicated SDK exists for real-time avatar sessions.
developerItems.evidenceText: The streaming-avatar SDK docs describe an SDK package for controlling live avatar sessions programmatically.

commercialItems.value: 4K export begins on Pro and continues on Business and Enterprise.
commercialItems.evidenceText: The pricing comparison maps 4K export to Pro and higher tiers.

commercialItems.value: Brand kit is included on Creator and higher tiers.
commercialItems.evidenceText: Brand kit appears in the pricing comparison and on localization feature content.

commercialItems.value: Interactive video, branching, quizzes, SCORM export, and LMS integrations are business or enterprise motions.
commercialItems.evidenceText: Those items appear in the Learning & Development and Business feature rows on the pricing page.

limitationItems.value: Agent, Avatar IV, and lip-synced translation are trial or premium-gated on lower tiers.
limitationItems.evidenceText: The free plan section explicitly frames these as trial premium features, while paid tiers describe larger premium usage pools.

limitationItems.rawLabel: API and web plans are separate
limitationItems.value: A paid web tier does not by itself imply API credits or API usage entitlement at the same billing pool.
limitationItems.evidenceText: HeyGen's API pricing docs explicitly say API plans are standalone subscriptions separate from standard HeyGen plans.

limitationItems.value: API free output includes a watermark.
limitationItems.evidenceText: The API subscription article calls out watermark inclusion on the free API plan and no watermark on paid API plans.

useCaseItems.fit: Strong fit when the same asset must be translated across many markets while keeping lip sync and brand terms aligned.
useCaseItems.evidenceText: The localization page emphasizes batch translation, captions, brand voice, and lip sync for global rollout.

useCaseItems.fit: Strong fit for narrated training or simulation use cases that benefit from an on-screen presenter or interactive avatar.
useCaseItems.evidenceText: The streaming API docs explicitly mention training simulations and virtual assistants; the pricing page adds SCORM and LMS flows.

useCaseItems.fit: API access plus templates and batch generation make it suitable for personalized outbound or onboarding videos.
useCaseItems.evidenceText: The API docs highlight SaaS integrations, personalized videos, and automation scenarios.

userFitItems.fit: Good fit for solo creators who care about avatars, voice cloning, and brand consistency more than deep editing control.
userFitItems.rationale: Creator and Pro tiers expose avatar-heavy premium features while remaining single-seat products.
userFitItems.evidenceText: Single-seat plans include unlimited avatar videos, voice cloning, and brand kit before business collaboration features appear.

userFitItems.fit: Business is oriented to teams that need seats, collaboration, and SSO without going full enterprise.
userFitItems.rationale: Business adds seat expansion, team management, collaboration, and centralized billing.
userFitItems.evidenceText: Those controls are concentrated in the Business feature list.

userFitItems.fit: Enterprise fit increases when access control, SCIM, multi-workspace governance, and success support matter.
userFitItems.rationale: The enterprise tier adds SCIM, granular access controls, commercial terms, and dedicated support.
userFitItems.evidenceText: These capabilities appear only in higher security and enterprise rows.

rawEvidenceBlocks.evidenceText: The public pricing table distinguishes creator, pro, business, and enterprise by export resolution, seats, collaboration, security, and premium usage pools.
Quick-start materials split HeyGen integrations into direct API and MCP, each with different authentication and billing routes.
Avatar docs describe multiple avatar types and explicitly allow engine choice between avatar III and avatar IV.
The translation API supports one or many target languages and returns translation identifiers for later polling.
Localization pages group batch translation, brand kit, captions, voice cloning, and lip sync into one workflow rather than treating subtitles as a side feature.

unresolvedQuestions.question: Does HeyGen expose a public administrator page for enabling or disabling individual foundation models or providers?
unresolvedQuestions.reason: Reviewed official pages mention advanced AI models and engine choices, but not a public provider governance screen like model marketplaces.

unresolvedQuestions.question: What are the exact feature boundaries between current LiveAvatar plans and older Interactive Avatar references?
unresolvedQuestions.reason: The docs include a migration notice and sunset warning, but a single current public plan matrix for LiveAvatar was not captured in this pass.
